response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong infrastructure and distributed systems experience, they fall significantly short on the hard minimum of 8 years of C++ programming (has roughly 1-2 years scattered across projects). Additionally, the role requires demonstrated technical leadership mentoring cloud/compute engineers, and this candidate's mentoring has been informal through workshops and code dojos rather than through a formal team leadership position. The candidate would need substantial C++ depth and proven technical leadership in a platform engineering context.
strengths: Extensive infrastructure and distributed systems experience including micro-services at scale (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby) and Kubernetes deployments
Proven commitment to engineering practices and code quality through TDD, CI/CD, and community mentoring via Refactoring Dojos and workshops
Full-stack capability spanning web, backend, embedded, and DevOps infrastructure across multiple roles
concerns: Significantly below 8-year C++ requirement with only ~1-2 years of C++ experience scattered across projects (NI LabWindows, robotic arms, sound processing)
No formal technical leadership experience leading engineering teams or mentoring engineers in a structured technical lead role
No demonstrated specialization in cloud compute platforms or infrastructure architecture beyond general cloud adoption (AWS CDK, Kubernetes)
